key_insight: A combined X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy and DFT workflow can accurately map the core, semi-core, and valence electronic structure of crystalline proteinogenic amino acids, with calculated binding energies matching experiment and enabling reliable spectral assignments.
engineering_relevance: This provides a validated framework for interpreting solid-state amino acid spectra, supporting better materials characterization in pharmaceutical, biological, and crystallographic applications.
method: Measured X-ray photoelectron spectra for 20 proteinogenic amino acids plus selenomethionine.
Computed core binding energies and projected densities of states using density functional theory.
Compared theory and experiment to assign peaks and interpret local bonding and crystal-structure effects.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement between calculated relative core binding energies and experiment across the amino-acid set, with DOS projections revealing systematic electronic-structure trends.
watch_item: Solid-state amino-acid XPS is experimentally challenging because radiation damage and spectral complexity can complicate acquisition and interpretation.
paper_title: A Comprehensive Experimental and Theoretical Spectroscopic Study of Proteinogenic Amino Acids
